全球通膨降溫趨勢與降息時程仍充滿不確定性，在經濟數據與聯準會政策訊號交錯影響下，下周市場將迎來多項關鍵指標與央行動態，成為牽動股匯市與利率預期的重要觀察窗口。

從周一開始，美國率先公布3月ISM服務業PMI，作為觀 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 全球通膨降溫與降息時程充滿不確定性，下周市場將迎來多項關鍵指標與央行動態。
• 美國將公布3月ISM服務業PMI、2月耐久財訂單、FOMC會議紀要、2月PCE價格指數等。
• 德國2月工廠訂單、南韓央行利率決議亦值得留意。
• 中國人民銀行預計公布3月份金融數據，信貸表現預計繼續回暖。
• 台股上市櫃公司3月、Q1營收將陸續於10日前公告完畢，將成為本周市場焦點。
news.Keywords: 通膨,降息,經濟數據,央行,財報

news.Title: 展望清明連假後行情 台股聚焦上市櫃公司Q1營收公布
news.Content: 台股2日開高走低且急轉翻黑，終場下跌602點或1.82％收32,572點，月線得而復失，且再度失守5日、10日及33K，展望清明連假後 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 台股2日開高走低下跌602點，失守5日、10日及33K，台指期夜盤反彈上漲372點。
• 需關注中東局勢動向與6日美股表現，將影響台股連假後走勢。
• 上市櫃公司3月、Q1營收將陸續於10日前公告完畢，將成為本周市場焦點。
• 上周三大法人大賣1549.37億元，外資賣超1601.26億元，投信買超249.73億元。
• 外資台指期未平倉空單水位回落，但仍維持高檔，短線反彈恐伴隨壓力。
news.Keywords: 台股,營收,財報,中東局勢,外資

news.Title: Jim Cramer Says “NVIDIA Deserves a Premium Price-to-Earnings Multiple”
news.Content: NVIDIA Corporation (NASDAQ: NVDA ) is among the stocks in focus, as Jim Cramer analyzed the broader market impact of the recent AI data center rally . Cramer ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Jim Cramer stated that NVIDIA (NVDA) deserves a premium price-to-earnings multiple, unlike memory companies like Micron (MU).
• Cramer expressed skepticism about the recent AI data center rally, noting that NVIDIA "really hasn’t done much of late."
• He contrasted NVIDIA's product innovation with memory companies that are "basically sold out and can just raise prices."
• Cramer believes NVIDIA's software ecosystem is a key differentiator.
news.Keywords: AI, data center, valuation, semiconductors, stock performance

news.Title: Jim Cramer Says “I Think Microsoft Gets Its Act Together, and I Am Hoping They Do”
news.Content: Microsoft Corporation (NASDAQ: MSFT ) is among the stocks in focus, as Jim Cramer analyzed the broader market impact of the recent AI data center rally . Cramer highlighted his worries around the stock, as he commented: Same goes for Amazon, Meta, Google, and Microsoft, which need data centers and can’t stop building them. Today wasn’t a ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Jim Cramer expressed significant worry about Microsoft's (MSFT) stock performance and direction, feeling the company has "lost its way."
• He noted that despite Microsoft's dominant position with Windows and a captive audience, the stock is struggling to move higher.
• Cramer questioned the value of Copilot compared to alternatives and expressed bafflement at Microsoft's apparent lack of concern.
• He admitted to a bad job owning Microsoft stock in his trust portfolio.
news.Keywords: Stock performance, AI, software, market sentiment, investor concerns

news.Title: TSMC vs. Nvidia: Which AI Supercycle Growth Stock Is the Better Long-Term Buy?
news.Content: The artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure boom has created some massive winners, and it's likely to keep minting winners well into the future. AI is perhaps the biggest technological shift the world has seen, and right now it's a race to see which companies will win. So if you think AI data center spending is set to soon peak, I'd think again. Two of the ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Nvidia (NVDA) has a dominant ~90% market share in GPUs, fueling the AI revolution with its hardware and CUDA software platform.
• TSMC (TSM) is essential for manufacturing advanced chips, holding a near-monopoly in advanced chip manufacturing with strong visibility into future demand.
• Customers are exploring alternatives to Nvidia, designing custom ASICs and partnering with AMD, which benefits TSMC.
• TSMC is well-positioned to benefit from trends in data center CPUs for agentic AI and autonomous vehicles.
news.Keywords: AI, semiconductors, GPUs, manufacturing, market share

news.Title: Prediction: Tesla Stock Is a Buy Before 2029 Due to This $10 Trillion Opportunity
news.Content: Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) is struggling when it comes to automotive sales. Last year, EV sales nationwide fell by roughly 2%. Tesla's auto deliveries, however, fell by around 9%. So in an already weak market, Tesla failed to keep pace. In response, Tesla's profits were nearly cut in half in 2025. The pain continued this week when the first-quarter sales were announced for 2026. Deliveries dropped 14% from the previous quarter, though they did rise modestly year over year. Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Tesla (TSLA) is facing struggles in automotive sales, with deliveries falling 9% last year and 14% in Q1 2026.
• However, Tesla's future growth is heavily tied to the robotaxi market, projected to be a $10 trillion opportunity by 2029.
• Ark Invest estimates Tesla's robotaxi business could represent ~90% of its enterprise value by 2029.
• Tesla's vertically integrated manufacturing and large installed base provide a scaling advantage for robotaxi deployment.
news.Keywords: Robotaxi, AI, autonomous driving, market opportunity, manufacturing

news.Title: 台廠搶攻ADAS 布局車用商機
news.Content: 全球車用電子加速邁向智慧化，先進駕駛輔助系統（ADAS）需求持續攀升，加上在消費性電子受制通膨影響，台灣IC設計業者加速車用市場布局，尋求新成長動能。聯發科、義隆電等在 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 台灣IC設計業者加速車用市場布局，尋求新成長動能，聯發科、義隆電等全面切入ADAS領域。
• 聯發科與DENSO合作開發ADAS車用系統單晶片，象徵由消費性電子跨足車用核心系統。
• 義隆電從感測與人機介面切入ADAS市場，並進入實質量產階段，Level 2+方案具延展性。
• 擷發科聚焦AI車載與自主載具系統，強調感測器融合與邊緣即時推論能力。
• Level 2+成為主流架構下，車用晶片需求持續擴大，台廠透過差異化策略掌握商機。
news.Keywords: 車用電子,ADAS,IC設計,聯發科,義隆電,AI

news.Title: Eyes on AI! Wedbush Keeps $600 Tesla Stock Price Target despite Q1 Miss
news.Content: Shares of Tesla (TSLA) dropped 5.42% on Thursday after the EV maker delivered 358,023 vehicles in Q1, missing Wall Street’s estimate of 370,000. Despite the miss, Wedbush’s five-star-rated analyst Dan Ives maintained his Buy rating ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Tesla (TSLA) missed Q1 delivery estimates (358,023 vehicles vs. 370,000 expected) and energy storage deployments (8.8 GWh vs. 14.4 GWh expected).
• Wedbush analyst Dan Ives maintained a Buy rating and $600 price target, citing Tesla's AI push and robotaxi plans as key drivers.
• Ives called Q1 deliveries an "underwhelming" start to the year, citing soft EV demand and regulatory hurdles in Europe for FSD approval.
• Tesla plans to invest about $20 billion in new factories for Cybercab, Optimus robot, battery production, and expanding AI computing capacity.
news.Keywords: EV sales, AI, robotaxi, delivery miss, price target

news.Title: How Much Would You Have if You Had Invested $2,000 In Apple When It Went Public?
news.Content: As difficult as this might be to digest, technology titan Apple (NASDAQ: AAPL) is now 50 years old. It reached the milestone on April 1, 2026. And what a run it's been! From its uncertain beginnings in Steve Jobs' parents' garage to near-bankruptcy in 1997 to the $3.8 trillion behemoth ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Apple (AAPL) is celebrating its 50th anniversary, having transformed from a garage startup to a $3.8 trillion behemoth.
• The introduction of the iPhone in 2007 revolutionized mobile technology, making smartphones central to people's lives.
• An initial $2,000 investment in Apple at its 1980 IPO would be worth over $5.18 million today.
• The majority of Apple's net gains have occurred since 2007, driven by its dominance in the smartphone industry.
news.Keywords: Stock performance, IPO, mobile technology, iPhone, market growth

news.Title: Arm Holdings (ARM) is a Winner, Says Jim Cramer
news.Content: Arm Holdings plc (NASDAQ:ARM) is one of the 10 Stocks Jim Cramer Talked About & Warned About A Weak Market . Chip designer Arm Holdings plc (NASDAQ:ARM)’s shares are up by 29% year-to-date ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Jim Cramer called Arm Holdings (ARM) a "winner," noting its shares are up 29% year-to-date and 22% over the past month.
• The stock closed 16% higher after Arm remarked its in-house AI chip could generate $15 billion in revenue by 2031.
• Bank of America increased its price target for Arm to $140, maintaining a Neutral rating, and expects a 25% rally by 2030.
• Cramer speculated in February that Arm would perform better in the AI market than initially believed.
news.Keywords: AI, chip design, revenue projection, stock performance, analyst rating

news.Title: Veteran analyst sends message to Tesla investors after Q1 miss
news.Content: Tesla ( TSLA ) stock just took a body blow after another underwhelming Q1 delivery report, but Dan Ives isn’t flinching. The veteran Wedbush analyst doubled down on his Buy rating for the stock, standing by a $600 price target , even after Tesla missed Wall Street 's delivery and energy storage expectations. That points to nearly 65% upside , and represents the highest rating on Wall Street for the EV giant. For perspective, at the time of writing, Tesla stock traded at $360.59 on Yahoo Finance . That leaves the stock down $20.67 a share , or nearly 5.4% ...

• Tesla (TSLA) missed Q1 delivery estimates (358,023 vehicles vs. 370,000 expected) and energy storage deployments (8.8 GWh vs. 14.4 GWh expected).
• Wedbush analyst Dan Ives maintained a Buy rating and $600 price target, citing Tesla's AI push and robotaxi plans as key drivers.
• Ives called Q1 deliveries an "underwhelming" start to the year, citing soft EV demand and regulatory hurdles in Europe for FSD approval.

news.Title: Prediction: AI Robotics Will Be a $375 Billion Industry and These 2 Stocks Will Lead It
news.Content: While some are patiently waiting for the artificial intelligence (AI) bubble to burst, many analysts continue to project that the market will experience incredible growth and revolutionize other industries along the way. Take AI robotics: According to some projections, this niche will be worth about $375.8 billion by 2035 and register a compound annual growth rate of 17.33% through then. Investors who identify the companies likely to lead this revolution could earn amazing returns along the way. Let's consider two candidates: Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) and ...
news.AI_Summary:
• AI robotics is projected to be a $375.8 billion industry by 2035, with a 17.33% CAGR.
• Tesla (TSLA) is shifting factory space from discontinued models to produce AI-powered Optimus humanoid robots, aiming for sales by late 2027.
• Amazon (AMZN) is increasing its use of AI-powered robots in warehouses and acquired Fauna Robotics, a humanoid robot startup.
• While Amazon may be lagging Tesla in robotics development, its cash balance and focus on innovation could allow it to catch up.
news.Keywords: AI robotics, humanoid robots, market growth, Tesla, Amazon

news.Title: Why Marvell Technology Rallied in March
news.Content: Shares of chipmaker Marvell Technology (NASDAQ: MRVL) rallied in March, rising 21.3% according to data from S&P Global Market Intelligence . Marvell had a busy month, reporting fourth-quarter earnings that beat expectations. Later in the month, the company also announced a landmark investment from and product collaboration with Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) . Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Marvell Technology (MRVL) shares rallied 21.3% in March, beating expectations with Q4 revenue of $2.2 billion (up 22.1% YoY) and adjusted EPS of $0.80.
• Marvell guided for a strong 9% sequential revenue jump in Q1, with data center revenue expected to grow 40% in fiscal 2027.
• Nvidia announced a $2 billion investment and product partnership with Marvell, focusing on integrating Marvell's solutions into Nvidia's NVLink Fusion ecosystem.
• This partnership aims to enable heterogeneous AI infrastructures and could lead to significant revenue for Marvell from customers like Microsoft.
news.Keywords: AI infrastructure, semiconductors, data center, partnership, revenue growth

news.Title: The S&P 500 Sank by 5% Last Month, but Here's Why This Super Semiconductor Stock Bucked the Sell-Off
news.Content: Ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East have sent the price of oil soaring, fueling fears of a slowdown in the U.S. economy. As a result, the S&P 500 (SNPINDEX: ^GSPC) stock market index plummeted by 5% in March, with many individual stocks suffering even steeper declines. But Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ: AMD) bucked the sell-off, delivering a gain of 2.5% for the month. Short-term moves in the stock market are usually just noise, especially during periods of heightened volatility. But AMD is gearing up for one of its ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) bucked the March market sell-off, gaining 2.5% while the S&P 500 fell 5%.
• AMD is preparing to ship its new MI450 GPUs combined with Helios, an integrated data center rack expected to deliver 36 times more performance than previous generations.
• Helios is expected to feature 50% more memory capacity than Nvidia's upcoming Vera Rubin GPU system.
• AMD's data center business generated a record $16.6 billion in revenue in 2025 (up 32% YoY), and the company expects this segment to grow 60% annually from 2026.
news.Keywords: AI, GPUs, data center, semiconductors, stock performance

news.Title: 2 AI Stocks That Survived the March Sell-Off -- and Look Stronger Because of It
news.Content: The March sell-off hit even the hottest areas of technology. However, not every tech stock was down for the month, and one pair of stocks in particular stood out as not only surviving the sell-off but coming out looking even stronger. That pair is Arm Holdings (NASDAQ: ARM) and Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ: AMD) , whose stocks both rose in March. The artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure market looks ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Arm Holdings (ARM) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stocks rose in March, outperforming the broader tech sell-off.
• The emergence of agentic AI is shifting the AI data center landscape towards high-performance CPUs, where Arm and AMD are well-positioned.
• Arm announced it would design its own CPU chips, targeting $15 billion in revenue from this new venture by 2031.
• AMD is the leader in data center CPUs and is poised to capture a large portion of the projected $100 billion server CPU market.
news.Keywords: Agentic AI, CPU, semiconductors, AI infrastructure, stock performance

news.Title: After Losing $700 Billion in Market Cap, Is Alphabet Stock a Buy?
news.Content: Big tech stocks have had a rough couple of months. Alphabet (NASDAQ: GOOG) (NASDAQ: GOOGL) hasn't escaped the sell-off and currently has a $3.5 trillion market cap after reaching $4.2 trillion earlier this year. That's basically the equivalent of an ExxonMobil being erased from ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Alphabet (GOOG/GOOGL) has seen its market cap drop from $4.2 trillion to $3.5 trillion, equivalent to erasing an ExxonMobil.
• Alphabet seamlessly transitioned into the AI era, solidifying Google Search as a top AI interaction point and Gemini as a widely used generative AI platform.
• Google Cloud's revenue rose 48% year over year in Q4, driven by its custom chips offering cost-performance benefits for AI applications.
• Alphabet plans $175 billion to $185 billion in capex for 2026, primarily for data center construction, which investors worry about ROI, but the company sees it as crucial for AI participation.
news.Keywords: AI, cloud computing, capital expenditure, market cap, stock valuation

news.Title: This Growth Stock Is Down 20% in the Great Rotation. I Think That's a Mistake.
news.Content: Artificial intelligence (AI) stocks have been leading the market higher for the past few years, but investors hit the emergency brake in 2026, sending many tech stocks suddenly tumbling. The move into value and small-cap stocks after years of underperformance by itself isn't necessarily a bad move. ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Micron Technology (MU) has been punished in the market rotation, trading at a forward P/E ratio under 4 times fiscal 2027 estimates, despite strong earnings and guidance.
• The DRAM market is experiencing a shortage, driving prices up, but investors question the sustainability of these dynamics.
• High-bandwidth memory (HBM) is crucial for AI chips, linking Micron's growth to AI data center build-outs.
• DRAM makers are signing longer-term contracts, suggesting a less cyclical market and a potential buying opportunity after the dip.
news.Keywords: AI, DRAM, HBM, semiconductor, stock valuation

news.Title: Why Meta's "Bellwether" Legal Loss Could Open up a Can of Worms
news.Content: Key Points Meta Platforms made headlines after courts in New Mexico and California issued legal rulings against the company. Despite paying damages in California that are a rounding error compared to Meta's financials, the case introduces real risk ahead. Still, Morgan Stanley remains confident in the stock, naming Meta a "top pick." Interested in Meta Platforms, Inc.? Here are five stocks we like better. The legal system just sent a shockwave through shares of the Magnificent Seven giant Meta Platforms (NASDAQ: META). The company lost two cases, one in New Mexico and another in California, leading shares to take significant ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Meta Platforms (META) lost two legal cases, facing $375 million in penalties in New Mexico and $4.2 million in damages from a California jury.
• The California ruling, finding social media apps defective for exploiting children, is seen as a "bellwether" case, potentially leading to thousands more similar lawsuits.
• These legal losses could amount to billions in damages, pressuring Meta's already substantial AI capital expenditures ($115-$135 billion for 2026).
• Meta plans to appeal both rulings, which could provide a defense against pending cases.
news.Keywords: Legal risk, AI, capital expenditure, stock sentiment, litigation

news.Title: 台積電卡「1瓶頸」輝達急縮產Rubin？「這廠」有望吃單受惠
news.Content:
財經中心／綜合報導

在AI晶片需求爆發下，台積電先進製程產能持續吃緊，甚至連核心客戶輝達（NVIDIA）也開始調整布局。韓媒指出，輝達已規劃下修明年AI加速器「Rubin Ultra」的生產量，並同步提高「Blackwell」平 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• AI晶片需求爆發，台積電先進製程產能持續吃緊，輝達(NVIDIA)規劃下修明年Rubin Ultra產量。
• 輝達將提高Blackwell平台比重，主因台積電3奈米產線高度集中，交期壓力浮現。
• 面對產能瓶頸，輝達調整產品組合，試圖維持出貨節奏與市場競爭力。
• 先進製程供不應求情況明顯，短期內將更依賴相對成熟的4奈米製程。
• 三星改善良率後，可能吸引客戶轉單，特斯拉、高通、超微等均與三星洽談合作。
news.Keywords: 台積電,輝達,AI晶片,產能吃緊,三星,先進製程

news.Title: Is Micron the New Nvidia?
news.Content: When OpenAI commercially released ChatGPT in late 2022, Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) was primarily seen as a graphics chip designer marketing its products toward computer gaming and secondarily as a chip provider for cryptocurrency mining operations. What most investors didn't realize was that the company's hardware was even more versatile than people had given it credit for. From 2023 to 2025, its chipsets became ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Micron Technology (MU) stock is up 27% year-to-date and nearly 309% over the last 12 months, driven by the memory and storage supercycle in AI.
• Micron supplies DRAM and NAND flash memory, which are becoming AI's latest bottleneck, with HBM being a key component for AI chips.
• Big tech hyperscalers are forecast to spend $700 billion on AI infrastructure in 2026, and Micron is sold out of its 2026 HBM inventory.
• While Micron is a key pillar in AI infrastructure, its application is more specialized than Nvidia's GPUs, making it unlikely to be the "new Nvidia" but enjoying an "Nvidia moment."
news.Keywords: AI, memory, HBM, semiconductor, stock performance

news.Title: 10年後感謝自己！內行點名「2檔神股」快買  擁有絕對定價權
news.Content: 在人工智慧（AI）快速發展、可能重塑數位世界之際，投資人若擔心過度集中於科技股，反而可將目光轉向實體產業與AI供應鏈企業。外媒點名半導體代工霸主台積電（TSMC）與國防航 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• AI發展重塑數位世界，外媒點名台積電(TSMC)與洛克希德馬丁(Lockheed Martin)為長期布局重點。
• 台積電掌握AI供應鏈關鍵地位，先進製程領先地位近乎壟斷，獲利能力優於多數輕資產軟體公司。
• 台積電加碼美國投資，長期投入1650億美元，看好AI帶動晶片需求擴張，獲利成長空間大。
• 洛克希德馬丁處於國防產業，具高度穩定性與抗景氣循環特性，全球地緣政治風險升溫帶動需求。
• 洛克希德馬丁在手訂單達1940億美元歷史新高，適合長期投資布局。
news.Keywords: AI,台積電,洛克希德馬丁,長期投資,供應鏈

news.Title: Should You Forget Sandisk and Buy These 2 AI Stocks While They're Cheap?
news.Content: Sandisk (NASDAQ: SNDK) was the best-performing stock within the Russell 1000 Index in the first quarter, with its share price surging 194%. The company benefited from a shortage in the NAND (flash) memory market, which helped drive up prices and led to huge revenue and earnings growth. While the NAND market is ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Sandisk (SNDK) was the best-performing stock in the Russell 1000 in Q1, surging 194% due to NAND flash memory shortages.
• While the NAND market benefits from AI infrastructure, it's historically cyclical, making Nvidia (NVDA) and Broadcom (AVGO) potentially better long-term AI stock picks.
• Nvidia's GPUs and CUDA software platform create a strong ecosystem for AI workloads, with attractive valuations (forward P/E of 21x current FY estimates).
• Broadcom leads in AI networking and custom AI chips (ASICs), with its valuation dropping to 17.5x next fiscal year's estimates due to explosive growth potential.
news.Keywords: AI, NAND flash memory, semiconductors, valuation, long-term investment

news.Title: Did Alphabet Just Say Checkmate to Micron?
news.Content: Alphabet (NASDAQ: GOOG) (NASDAQ: GOOGL) just dropped a bombshell that is sending waves through the stock market, especially in the memory chip sector. Before Alphabet's announcement, the assumption was that memory capacity for AI computing chips would be in a supply constraint for several years, leading to soaring memory prices. Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Alphabet's (GOOG/GOOGL) TurboQuant algorithm reduces AI memory needs by six times, potentially impacting memory chip stocks like Micron (MU) and Sandisk (SNDK).

news.Content: Last week, Alphabet 's (NASDAQ: GOOGL) (NASDAQ: GOOG) Google unveiled TurboQuant, an algorithm that marked a significant advancement in artificial intelligence (AI). news.AI_Summary:

• This breakthrough caused memory chip stocks like Micron (MU) and Sandisk (SNDK) to fall amid fears of reduced demand.
• The Jevons Paradox suggests that increased efficiency could lower costs and thus increase demand for memory chips, fueling AI adoption.

news.AI_Summary:

news.Title: US Lawmakers Propose Crackdown on Chip Tool Sales to China
news.Content: (Bloomberg) -- US lawmakers unveiled legislation that seeks to crack down on exports of chipmaking tools to China, especially from allies including the Netherlands and Japan, in a bipartisan effort to help Washington further constrain Beijing’s technology ambitions. The draft bill, introduced in the House on Thursday, would strengthen existing restrictions on semiconductor manufacturing equipment sales from firms like ASML Holding NV and Tokyo Electron Ltd. A companion version is expected to be released in the Senate later this month, according to people familiar with the matter. The goal is to subject these companies to the same, tougher curbs their ...
news.AI_Summary:
• US lawmakers have introduced legislation (MATCH Act) to crack down on chipmaking tool exports to China, including from allies like the Netherlands and Japan.
• The bill aims to strengthen existing restrictions on firms like ASML Holding NV and Tokyo Electron Ltd., subjecting them to tougher curbs faced by US rivals.
• This includes banning engineers from maintaining and repairing tools at certain Chinese facilities, impacting Applied Materials Inc., Lam Research Corp., and KLA Corp.
• The legislation seeks to align measures across US and foreign firms and broaden the scope of controlled technologies, potentially tightening restrictions for US firms as well.
news.Keywords: Export controls, China, semiconductors, chipmaking equipment, legislation

news.Title: Lumentum (LITE) to Open Greensboro Plant for AI Data Center Lasers
news.Content: Lumentum Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ: LITE ) is one of the best US stocks to buy and hold for the next 3 years . On March 26, Lumentum announced plans to ...
news.AI_Summary:

news.Title: Can INTC's Advancements in AI Inference Bolster Its Market Position?

news.Title: AMAT, LRCX: Bank of America Is Still Pounding the Table on These 2 Chip Stocks
news.Content: Middle East tensions linked to the Iran war have pressured several semiconductor equipment stocks, pushing them sharply lower. The situation has reignited concern about global supply chain fragility, particularly access to helium and other materials essential for chip production. This is forcing the market to rethink which companies can hold their ground if disruptions linger or even escalate. Bank of America is leaning into that debate by keeping two semiconductor equipment giants at the top of its preference list, despite the latest drawdowns. This stance suggests ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Bank of America maintains a positive outlook on Applied Materials (AMAT) and Lam Research (LRCX) despite recent semiconductor stock drawdowns due to Middle East tensions.
• AMAT is a $271.3B semiconductor equipment manufacturer with a forward P/E of 29.1x, collaborating with Micron (MU) on memory technology for AI.
• LRCX, a $266.8B semiconductor equipment company, is collaborating with IBM on sub-1 nm logic scaling, trading at a forward P/E of 40.80x.
• AMAT reported Q1 fiscal 2026 EPS of $2.39 (vs. $2.31 estimate) on revenue of $7.01B (up 3.1% YoY), with a "Strong Buy" rating and an average price target implying 12.6% upside.
news.Keywords: Semiconductor equipment, AI, stock valuation, analyst rating, collaboration

news.Title: Can TSM Meet FY26 Margin Guidance Amid Fab Expansion Cost Pressures?
news.Content: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company TSM, also known as TSMC, has consistently delivered strong gross margins over the past few quarters despite mounting cost pressures from overseas fab expansion. news.AI_Summary:

news.Title: 記憶體飆漲 手機晶片開「砍單第1槍」！專家曝這2產品下一波受害
news.Content:
記憶體報價狂飆觸發全球消費電子警報。美系外資示警，受限於記憶體成本激增，2026年全球智慧型手機出貨量面臨顯著的下修壓力，整體消費需求將受到抑制。財經專家阮慕驊分析，中東戰火已對全球景氣產生負面衝擊，而且拖愈久影響愈大，手機晶片已出現「砍單潮」，下一波是否延燒至PC與NB（筆電）市場？

記憶體漲價壓力 手機需求轉弱

阮慕驊在臉書發文表示，記憶體價格大漲正直接衝擊消費端需求。美系外資觀察，中國大陸市場手機晶片庫存已率先修正，在成本墊高情況下，智慧型手機需求逐步受到壓抑，連帶影響上游供應鏈。

他指出，包括聯發科與高通已開始下修4奈米訂單，預估減少約2萬至3萬片晶圓，約相當於晶片出貨量減少1500萬至2000萬顆；此外，相關測試供應商如京元電也出現類似砍單情況。不過，由於AI與網通需求強勁，相關產能空缺已迅速被填補，對台積電影響有限。

PC與NB需求是否受波及 市場下一波關注焦點

外資同步下修今年全球手機出貨量，預估年減13%，其中Android陣營衰退幅度達15%，至於iPhone，則因新品發表時程調整，部分機型可能由今年秋季延後至明年春季推出，影響全年出貨表現。

阮慕驊指出，外資已將聯發科手機出貨預估下修至年減2成，並調降京元電第二季營收成長預期，由原先季增15%下修至10%。他示警，手機晶片砍單已經出現，後續是否擴及PC與筆電（NB）市場，值得密切關注。

他並強調，AI伺服器與雲端服務業者對記憶體價格上漲承受力較高，但消費端對價格敏感度明顯不同。在中東戰事與通膨壓力交織下，整體物價上漲趨勢已逐步擴散，市場不宜過度期待fed降息，「不升息」恐已是對股市最有利的情境。

news.Title: 【Follow法人】面板不同調！外資棄群創拱友達 主動式ETF00981A成寵兒掃貨6.6萬張
news.Content:
（下午5時16分更新外資買賣超個股）

台股今（1）日勁揚1451.83點，收在33174.82點。三大法人聯袂買超549.82億元，其中外資回補232.51億元，終結連續4賣，主要轉買超主動統一台股增長（00919）6.6萬張；記憶體股華邦電（2344）和力積電（6770）則逆勢遭調節。分析師表示，外資買超金額不算多，僅能稱為「踩剎車」，其仍在評估後續局勢的發展，尚未出現明確的長線回補訊號。

外資今日買超前三大：主動統一台股增長、台新新光金、友達

主動統一台股增長（00919）今年來表現搶眼，吸引資金大舉湧入，外資今終結連3賣，轉買超6萬6,509張居台股買超之冠；主動復華未來50（00991A）也有3萬904張買超。

外資主要買超鋼鐵塑化股，包括中鋼（2002）1萬8,820張、南亞（1303）1萬6,451張以及台化（1326）9,229張。

外資今日賣超前三大：華邦電、力積電、群創

憶體族群今日隨著美光止跌反彈，然而，外資逆勢砍華邦電（2344）3萬628張，以及力積電（6770）2萬6,479張。盤面亮眼的面板族群則是不同調，外資出脫群創（3481）1萬3,926張，買超友達（2409）3萬3,792張。

外資僅買233億元 分析師：「踩剎車」未翻多

根據證交所公布籌碼動向，三大法人今日合計買超549.82億元，其中外資買超232.51億元，終結連續4賣。外資前4個交易日共調節台股達1886.58億元。

投信續站買方，買超101.1萬元。自營商合計買超216.19億元，其中自行買賣部分買超67.6億元，避險方面買超148.58億元。

證期雙照分析師翁偉捷受訪表示，台股今日大漲逾千點，如此鉅大漲幅，外資照理應買超七、八百億元，但今日回補的量不算多，顯示其對台股的後續表現仍存有疑慮，僅能視為短期的「踩剎車」，而非全面看好回歸。

翁偉捷說明，外資持觀望態度的主要原因在於荷姆茲海峽封鎖時間的長短，對全球經濟持續產生影響。此外，根據美國總統川普最新的說法，戰爭可能還需至少兩至三周才能結束，這使得外資在作邏輯上，目前在全球資產部位仍傾向以賣超為主，對台股的買盤力道因此受限。

翁偉捷續指，外資今日動作偏向，在短期內尋找過去表現較強勢的個股進行局部布局，觀察是否有短期機會，並等待時機再進行後續的大規模回補。

王詩婷／財經特派記者，主跑勞動部、半導體、生技等
